agent_runs.final_text:
This week I'm passing. 

**Summary of my session:**
- Pulled bankroll: full $100 (10,000c) cash, no open positions, all risk limits fresh.
- Surveyed the live in-season series: MLB, WNBA, and various futures.
- Investigated candidates deeply: today's WNBA games (Dallas/Connecticut, Seattle/Phoenix), the MLB game-winner slate, and season-long futures (World Series champion, NL East). 
- **Every single market returned null prices with completely empty orderbooks.**

With no market prices, there's nothing to measure an edge against, and with no resting liquidity, any limit order I place would just expire unfilled. Betting into that would be guessing, not edge-hunting — and my rules say a week with no tradeable edge is a week to pass. I've preserved the full bankroll and will re-engage next week when markets are priced and liquid.

Weekly session complete. I placed no new bets.

Summary: I surveyed MLB, WNBA, tennis/Wimbledon, F1/NASCAR, and Nathan’s markets. The markets that looked potentially interesting had no quoted prices and empty orderbooks, so there was no reliable market-implied probability or realistic fill path. I’m keeping the existing Joey Chestnut and Miki Sudo Nathan’s positions and preserving cash rather than forcing illiquid orders without a clear edge.

I have completed my weekly session. My limit for new positions is currently at 0, and after checking the orderbooks for my 5 open positions, I found no liquidity to warrant adding to them. I recorded a note summarizing this and will stand pat for this week, letting my existing positions play out. See you next week!
